description: Australian organised crime group based in Western Sydney, led by Rafat Alameddine. Allegedly one of the largest drug-trafficking organisations in Sydney, earning around $1 million weekly profit at its peak. Gained notoriety in the early 2020s Sydney gangland war due to a feud with the Hamzy/Hamze crime family. Members have been linked to terrorism-related activities.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 10 April 2025, seven alleged associates of the Alameddine crime network abducted an unidentified 27 year old businessman and his unidentified 29 year old girlfriend in Sunshine Coast, Queensland...
country_links.context: The Alameddine crime network operates in Australia, with members arrested, involved in kidnappings, shootings, and other criminal activities across New South Wales and Queensland.

country_links.evidence_quote: Rafat Alameddine left Sydney a free man, and travelled to live in Lebanon
country_links.context: The organization's leader traveled to and lived in Lebanon, and other members joined him there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Masood Zakaria's escape from Sydney to Turkey, via Perth and Malaysia, was facilitated by Fawaz Elmir...
country_links.context: The Alameddine network routed an associate's escape through Malaysia.

country_links.evidence_quote: Masood Zakaria's escape from Sydney to Turkey, via Perth and Malaysia, was facilitated by Fawaz Elmir, who was based in Lebanon at the time, at a cost of between $500,000 and $750,000.
country_links.context: The Alameddine network facilitated an associate's escape to Turkey.
